Dear Mr. Ahu.ia:
I would like to request confirnation of our discussion regardinE
advice on a possible conflict of interest on the part of a rnember
of the Prison Industry Board. The Board is nade up of appointees
of the Governor and the Legislature. 
It 
sets policy for and
oversees Lhe operation of the Prison Industry Authority (PIA).
As we discussed, one of the Prison Industry Board members has
strongly supported the concept of the Prison Industry Authority
using a Manufacturers Representative firm to sell its products to
Iocal governmental agencies. PIA recently issued a Request for
Proposals and selected the firnr of Electrical Supply of Salinas
to carry out this project. 
Mr. Zitzke was not involved in the
selection of the firn.
Because of his interest in . this area, however, Mr. Zitzke is
considering resigni-ng from the Board in order to serve aa a
consulta.nt to Electrical 
SuppLy of Salinas and narket PIA
products to local governmental agencies, 
We understand that if
he were to do this, 
he would be prohibited from representing
Electrical Supply of Salinas before the Board and fron assisting
them in formulatinE another contract with PIA.
We also understand that there would be a conflict of interest if,
at the time of the contract, he knew or expected that he would
receive income from Electrical Supply of SaLinas.
It 
should be noted that the Board did not vote on either
establishing the overall progran or selecting the individual

sections.question: Whether a Prison Industry Board member (Mr. Zitzke) would have a conflict of interest if he resigned from the Board to serve as a consultant to Electrical Supply of Salinas, which was recently selected by the Prison Industry Authority to sell its products to local governmental agencies. The request seeks confirmation regarding: (1) whether Mr. Zitzke would be prohibited from representing Electrical Supply before the Board or assisting in formulating another contract with PIA; and (2) whether there would be a conflict of interest if he knew or expected to receive income from Electrical Supply at the time of the contract.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a Prison Industry Board member violate conflict of interest laws by resigning to become a consultant for a firm that was selected by the Authority to represent its products, and what restrictions would apply to his future dealings with the Board?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a state agency seeking FPPC advice, not an FPPC response letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question is presented in narrative form within the body of the letter. The document lacks the typical structure of FPPC advice letters with explicitly marked Q/C sections.

The request seeks confirmation regarding: (1) whether Mr. Zitzke would be prohibited from representing Electrical Supply before the Board or assisting in formulating another contract with PIA; and (2) whether there would be a conflict of interest if he knew or expected to receive income from Electrical Supply at the time of the contract.

embedding.summary: The Prison Industry Board's Executive Officer requests written FPPC confirmation regarding potential conflict of interest issues for board member Wayne Zitzke, who is considering resigning to serve as a consultant to Electrical Supply of Salinas, a firm recently selected by the Prison Industry Authority. The letter outlines the facts, including that Zitzke was not involved in selecting the firm and seeks clarification on prohibited activities and timing of the conflict.
